emergency.spill_response: Avoid creating dust. Collect spilled material carefully and place in a suitable container.
emergency.fire_fighting_method: Boric acid is non-combustible. Use extinguishing media appropriate for surrounding materials.

environmental_information.aquatic_toxicity: Low to moderate toxicity at high concentrations

regulatory_information.osha_status: Hazardous chemical due to reproductive toxicity classification

basic_information.chemical_type: Organic Acid, Carboxylic Acid, Weak Acid

physical_properties.solubility_in_other_solvents: Soluble in ethanol; slightly soluble in ether

hazard_statements: H319: Causes serious eye irritation.
precautionary_statements: P264: Wash hands thoroughly after handling.
P280: Wear protective gloves and eye protection.
P305+P351+P338: IF IN EYES: Rinse cautiously with water for several minutes.
safety_information.corrosive: No (weak acid; concentrated solutions may irritate)
safety_information.reactive: Yes (reacts with strong bases and oxidizers)

ppe.respirator: Dust mask if powder becomes airborne

storage.storage_temperature: Store at room temperature in a cool, dry place
storage.container_type: Tightly closed plastic or glass container

first_aid.skin_contact: Wash skin with plenty of water. Remove contaminated clothing if irritation occurs.
first_aid.eye_contact: Flush eyes with water for at least 15 minutes. Seek medical attention if irritation persists.
first_aid.inhalation: Move to fresh air if dust is inhaled. Seek medical advice if symptoms continue.
first_aid.ingestion: Rinse mouth with water. Seek medical advice if large amounts are consumed.

emergency.spill_response: Avoid creating dust. Sweep up carefully and place in a suitable container. Clean contaminated area with water.
emergency.fire_fighting_method: Use extinguishing media suitable for surrounding fire.
